extra.reasoning: The mutual-exclusion property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is violated. rec_drug_a is only ever set true by r2 (priority 50), whose condition requires bradycardia AND anticoagulated AND low_ejection_fraction AND asthma. rec_drug_b is only ever set true by r5 (priority 50), whose condition is simply bradycardia. Since r2's condition is a strict superset that includes bradycardia, any input that fires r2 (making rec_drug_a true) necessarily satisfies bradycardia, which also fires r5 (making rec_drug_b true). There are no competing rules for either output, so neither default overrides them. Counterexample: bradycardia=true, anticoagulated=true, low_ejection_fraction=true, asthma=true (other three inputs false, as they only affect rec_drug_c). This yields rec_drug_a=true via r2 and rec_drug_b=true via r5, jointly violating the property.
